page: Amazon Web Services c7a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU bundled with this instance. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2817.37 placing it in the top 25 percent. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small 135M models at 355.9 tokens per second, alongside strong prompt processing at 3048.53 tokens per second. Other workloads, such as multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, fall within the average range.

Given its dedicated core allocation and high-speed DDR5 memory, the c7a.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density for compute-bound tasks without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is well-suited for workloads requiring high single-thread performance, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference. However, the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, which should be factored into the overall architectural design.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU capabilities and top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads, text processing, and lightweight inference tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high-speed memory.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for single-threaded and lightweight inference workloads.
